Readme

Purpose: rebuild the local multi-file static documentation site in G:\Docs\_site.

Quick Start

  1. Place source documents under G:\Docs\TTIS, G:\Docs\TSG, G:\Docs\Pursuite, or G:\Docs\DocGuardAI.
  2. Open PowerShell in G:\Docs.
  3. Run the build script.
cd G:\Docs
& "C:\Users\echar\.cache\codex-runtimes\codex-primary-runtime\dependencies\python\python.exe" build_site.py

What It Builds

Output Description
_site\index.html The tabbed shell with TTIS, TSG, Pursuite, and DocGuardAI navigation.
_site\<tab>\*.html One generated page for every source .md or .html document.
_site\assets\css\styles.css Shared site styles plus embedded Pygments syntax highlighting CSS.
_site\assets\js\app.js Hash routing and tab/sub-nav behavior.
_site\build-report.md Source counts, conversion status, Unsorted warnings, and skipped files.

Rules

  • Tab assignment is based only on the top-level folder under G:\Docs.
  • .md files are converted to HTML at build time with tables, fenced code, footnotes, table of contents, attributes, and code highlighting enabled.
  • Existing .html files are ingested by extracting body content and stripping page-level scripts/styles.
  • The script rewrites internal links between generated documents and copies referenced local images into _site\assets\img.
  • The script deletes and recreates _site on each run, so re-running is clean and idempotent.
  • Files already inside _site are never re-ingested.

Admin & Ops Living Source

  1. Edit the Markdown source named TTIS_ADMIN_OPERATIONS_GUIDE.md.
  2. Place it in G:\Docs\TTIS.
  3. Run build_site.py.
  4. The build pins the Admin & Ops tab to the Markdown-derived page, refreshes G:\Docs\TTIS\TTIS_ADMIN_OPERATIONS_GUIDE.html, then moves the Markdown source to G:\Docs\_source_originals\TTIS\TTIS_ADMIN_OPERATIONS_GUIDE.md.orig.
  5. For the next edit, place a fresh updated .md copy back into G:\Docs\TTIS and rebuild.

After Running

  • Open G:\Docs\_site\index.html by double-clicking it.
  • Review G:\Docs\_site\build-report.md after every build.
  • If the report lists Unsorted files, move them into one of the four project folders and run the script again.
